lesson.title: Special Factoring Formulas
lesson.body: Three patterns appear often and should be recognized instantly: **Difference of squares**: a² − b² = (a + b)(a − b). **Perfect square trinomial**: a² + 2ab + b² = (a + b)² and a² − 2ab + b² = (a − b)². **Sum/difference of cubes**: a³ + b³ = (a + b)(a² − ab + b²) and a³ − b³ = (a − b)(a² + ab + b²). Memory trick for cubes: SOAP — Same sign, Opposite sign, Always Positive.

rooms.question: Factor: x² − 81
rooms.explanation: x² − 81 = x² − 9² = (x+9)(x−9). Difference of squares: a²−b² = (a+b)(a−b). The (x−9)² error is a perfect square, which equals x²−18x+81, not x²−81.

rooms.question: Factor: 9x² − 25
rooms.explanation: 9x² = (3x)² and 25 = 5². Difference of squares: (3x+5)(3x−5). Check: (3x)²−5² = 9x²−25 ✓. The (9x+5)(x−5) error doesn't correctly take the square root of 9x².

rooms.question: Factor: x² + 12x + 36
rooms.explanation: Check: a²=x², 2ab=2(x)(6)=12x ✓, b²=36 ✓. This is a perfect square: (x+6)². The (x+6)(x−6) error gives x²−36, not x²+12x+36. When the constant is a perfect square and the middle term is exactly 2√(constant)×x, it's a perfect square trinomial.

rooms.question: Factor: x³ − 8
rooms.explanation: x³ − 8 = x³ − 2³. Difference of cubes: (a−b)(a²+ab+b²) = (x−2)(x²+2x+4). Note: the second factor x²+2x+4 does NOT factor further. The (x+2) option would be sum of cubes (a+b)(a²−ab+b²) = (x+2)(x²−2x+4) for x³+8.

rooms.question: Identify which special pattern applies to each expression:
rooms.explanation: Difference of squares: two perfect square terms subtracted (a²−b²). Perfect square trinomial: middle term = 2×(square root of each end). Sum of cubes: a³+b³. Difference of cubes: a³−b³. Recognizing the pattern first saves using the AC method.

boss.name: The Difference Specter
boss.taunt: Difference of squares. Sum of your failures.

questions.question: Factor completely: 2x³ − 50x
questions.explanation: First factor GCF: 2x(x²−25). Then factor x²−25 as difference of squares: 2x(x+5)(x−5). Always factor completely — leaving 2x(x²−25) is partially factored. Check: 2x(x+5)(x−5) = 2x(x²−25) = 2x³−50x ✓.

questions.question: Factor: 8x³ + 27
questions.explanation: 8x³ = (2x)³ and 27 = 3³. Sum of cubes: (a+b)(a²−ab+b²) = (2x+3)((2x)²−(2x)(3)+3²) = (2x+3)(4x²−6x+9). SOAP: Same sign (+), Opposite sign (−), Always Positive. The difference of cubes version would have (2x−3)(4x²+6x+9).

questions.question: Factor: x⁴ − 16
questions.explanation: x⁴−16 = (x²)²−4². Difference of squares: (x²+4)(x²−4). Then x²−4 = (x+2)(x−2) (another difference of squares). Final: (x²+4)(x+2)(x−2). Note x²+4 cannot be factored over the reals (no real square root of −4).
